instructions:
Write Episode 2 ('Career') of the 4-episode 专题 series on Evan Glass (Montgomery County Executive, Democratic, district MoCo). Air date: 2026-05-21.

EPISODE 2 SCOPE — Career:
Professional history end-to-end, in chronological order, with employer + start/end years for every role. Public-facing work: media, op-eds, books, speeches, podcast interviews, X/Twitter and LinkedIn back to earliest archived posts. Court records, professional licensure, board memberships, charity work. Net worth and disclosed conflicts of interest.

MUST COVER (do not skip):
  • every job held with employer and dates
  • military service if any
  • boards / nonprofits / volunteer leadership
  • public statements outside politics (op-eds, books, podcast guest spots)
  • social media history — earliest posts you can find
  • court records (PACER + state) if any
  • publicly-disclosed financial picture

DOSSIER (READ FIRST): `C:\Users\evan_\local_politic_scan\data\candidate_dossiers\evan-glass.md` — this is the source of truth for biographical / career / political facts. Cite from it. If a section you need for episode 2 is missing or thin, STOP and write a fresh `candidate_dossier` brief into the inbox for this candidate before writing today's script.

FORMAT:
  • Two hosts ALEX (presenter) and JORDAN (curious co-host).
  • Every line starts `ALEX:` or `JORDAN:` — no stage directions,     no markdown, no emoji, no '[laughs]', no asterisks.
  • LENGTH TARGET — 3900 words (±300; ~30 min at 130 wpm).
  • Cite specific names, dates, employers, schools, bill numbers,     URLs from the dossier — generalities are why earlier drafts     felt thin.

PM LENGTH CALIBRATION:
  • No prior scripts to calibrate against. Aim for ~3900 words (~30 min).
  • Hit the target. If you finish the must-cover list early,     extend the JORDAN follow-up questions and add depth to the     biographical reads — never pad with filler.

AVOID — do not reuse any framing on `context.avoid_list`. The PM agent has flagged these as already-shipped this week:
  - "A listener asked us last week which Democratic primary candidates actually oppose sanctuary policy" as a setup line — used verbatim in 2026-04-28 ep1 and 2026-04-29 ep3
  - Evan Glass background-screening DHS bill as the sanctuary-policy example — used in 2026-04-28 ep1 and 2026-04-29 ep3
  - Montgomery County Council ICE-agent mask-ban ordinance as the sanctuary benchmark — used in 2026-04-29 ep2 (may be referenced descriptively but NOT as the framing hook)
  - "Two stories, properly worked through" / "not spreading thin across six" as an episode-framing device — used in 2026-04-29 ep1
  - Governor Moore's "Leave No One Behind" slogan contrasted with disability service cuts — used in 2026-04-27 ep1 and ep1.rewrite
  - ACA marketplace quiet-downgrade story ("thousands of Marylanders have been quietly downgrading") — used in 2026-04-28 ep1 and revisited 2026-04-29 ep3
  - Maryland's 90-day part-time legislature explainer ("they meet in Annapolis for ninety days, the rest of the time they're lawyers, teachers, small business owners") — used in 2026-04-26 ep3
  - The wave of 25 Maryland lawmakers retiring or running for other offices framing — used in 2026-04-26 ep1 and ep4
  - Beltsville Agricultural Research Center closure as a District 19 candidate-record hook — used in 2026-04-27 ep4
  - Listener's own frustration about promised deep-dives not delivering, narrated back to the listener — has shipped across at least five episodes; do not use as a framing device again

LISTENER POSITIONING:
  • Locale: Rockville, Montgomery County, Maryland.
  • Their districts:
  - U.S. Congressional District: MD-8
  - MD Legislative District: 19
  - MD State Senate District: 19
  - MD House of Delegates District: 19
  - Senatorial District: 19
  - County Councilmanic District: 7
  - Election District: 8
  - Precinct: 08-008
  - Circuit Court District: 6
  - Appellate Circuit Court: 7
  - School District: 005
  - Party Central Committee: MCC19
  • The listener is a registered Democrat in Rockville, MD,     primary on June 23, 2026. Speak as though we already know     their districts; never tell them to 'look up their ballot'.
  • If any item from `context.listener_notes` ties into this     candidate, address it by name in the dialogue.

Write the full dialogue to `output_file`. Plain text only. If the dossier doesn't have the facts you need, do NOT invent — have a host say on-air 'we don't have a public record of X yet' and move on.
